question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 91.00% on 2025-09-24 for the Metaculus question "Will Zohran Mamdani be elected Mayor of New York City in 2025?"?
summary: Meta-prediction on whether Metaculus CP for Mamdani NYC mayor will exceed 91% on Sep 24. Current CP is 0.90, stable for 10 consecutive readings since Sep 7 after dropping from 0.93 in early September. Historical analysis shows that when CP is at 0.90, it's above 0.91 at +10 readings only ~33% of the time. Recent downward momentum (0.93→0.90) and stability at the current level with 133 forecasters suggest the CP is more likely to remain at or near 0.90 than climb back above 91%.

factors.description: Current CP is 0.90, below the >0.91 threshold — status quo favors NO
CP has been stable at 0.90 for 10 consecutive readings (Sep 7-14), suggesting stickiness
Recent trend is downward: CP dropped from 0.93 to 0.90 over Sep 5-7
Historical transition analysis: from 0.90, CP is >0.91 at +10 readings 33% of the time
CP was above 91% just 10 days ago (Sep 1-6), showing it can reach the threshold
133 forecasters makes CP moderately stable and resistant to individual forecast changes
No obvious news catalyst between now and Sep 24 to push CP either direction

reasoning:
This is a meta-prediction question about CP trajectory. Let me gather the essential data.Now let me analyze the CP trajectory data carefully.**Forecast: 30% probability (NO lean)**

Key reasoning:

- **Current CP: 0.90** — sitting just below the >0.91 threshold, stable for 10 consecutive readings since Sep 7
- **Recent downward trend**: CP dropped from 0.93 (early Sep) to 0.90 over a few days and has stayed there
- **Historical transition rates**: When CP is at 0.90, it's above 0.91 at +10 readings only ~33% of the time; when it exits long 0.90 streaks, it goes above 0.91 only 2/7 times (29%)
- **133 forecasters** makes the CP relatively stable and resistant to sharp moves
- **Counterpoint**: CP was above 91% just 10 days ago, so it's not far from the threshold — but the trend and stickiness at 0.90 favor NO
